original_submission.content: "Government covers 100% of energy subsidies for low-income households."

source_credibility.name: The Straits Times
source_credibility.description: Maintains high editorial standards with a robust fact-checking process. Known for objective reporting on regional policy and legislative changes.

evidence_analysis.content: Official press release from the Ministry of Finance confirms the subsidy framework and the Q3 budget allocation mentioned in the claim.
Cross-verified by major economic networks including Bloomberg and CNBC, noting consistent detail regarding the tiered rebate structure.

claims_breakdown.title: Verified: Energy Subsidies
claims_breakdown.description: Legislation for energy relief was passed in the Q3 budget review.

claims_breakdown.title: Context Needed: 100% Coverage
claims_breakdown.description: Full coverage is only applicable to specific heating costs, not total energy.

context_summary.title: Context Summary
context_summary.content: While the energy subsidy program exists, the claim of **"100% coverage"** is an exaggeration. Current legislation establishes a *tiered rebate system* where only the most vulnerable households receive up to 90% of heating costs, while others receive between 30-50%. Recent budget debates suggested a 100% pilot for three specific districts, which likely caused the confusion.
